properties.description: A machine tool, with a horizontal axis, for the machining of metallic materials by means of cold chip removal. Models: Art. T999/230V –T999/230V3A, Art. T999/400V -T999/400V3A.
properties.spec: Centres distance: 1000mm; Spindle hole diameter: 38mm; Maximum swing over the bed: 320mm; Weight: 520kg; Voltage: 230V/50Hz or 400V/50Hz; Motor power: 1500W/1800W.

properties.description: Installed on the head shaft, supported by two precision bearings. It is self-centring, with three terminals (jaws) for clamping the workpiece. Its rotation provides the cutting motion.
properties.spec: Spindle hole diameter: 38mm; Spindle diameter (3+3 self centring): 160mm; Spindle speed: 70-2000 RPM.

properties.description: Slides along the rails of the bed, used to hold, position and feed the tool. Equipped with an independent lubrication system.
A cast iron body that ensures rigidity and stability. Equipped with a locking system on the lathe guides and an adjustment screw for axial alignment.
properties.spec: Tailstock sleeve diameter: 32mm; Maximum travel of the tailstock sleeve: 80mm.

properties.description: Houses electrical controls including the main switch, emergency button, coolant pump selector, and gears application button. Powered by 24V DC voltage.
A red mushroom-shaped button on the electric panel. Pressing it stops the motion of the electric motor and other rotating parts in an emergency.
Screens and guards (fixed or movable) designed to protect the operator from coolant, chips, splinters, tool fragments, or workpiece projection. Movable guards are interlocked with safety switches.

relations.target: Tampering with Safety Devices is Forbidden

properties.description: Equipment such as goggles, gloves, overalls/aprons, and safety shoes that operators must ALWAYS wear to prevent accidents and injuries.
The process of lubricating and greasing the machine's moving parts before starting operation and as part of periodic maintenance to ensure smooth function and prevent wear.
A critical assembly and commissioning task involving the use of a precision spirit level to ensure the lathe table and rails are perfectly level, preventing deformation and vibrations.
Components and measures within the electrical circuit to prevent electric shock and ensure safe operation, including a rotary main switch with safety key and grounding conductor.
Compulsory training and instruction for workers responsible for using the machine, covering conditions of use, foreseeable abnormal situations, and safety procedures.
A hazard that can result from improper connection of the Lathe and/or the grounding conductor, or tampering with the electrical system.

id: Tampering with Safety Devices is Forbidden

properties.description: A strict prohibition against modifying or removing guards, safety devices, labels, and information plates on the machine, as it can lead to injury.
A critical safety action requiring the power cord to be unplugged from the power outlet before performing maintenance, registration, tool replacement, moving, or cleaning operations.
The material being machined on the lathe, rotated by the spindle and shaped by the tool.
Belts that transmit power from the motor, requiring periodic tension adjustment.
Components within the gearbox that allow for different spindle speeds and feed speeds, requiring slack adjustment.
Unwanted oscillations or shaking of the machine, which can affect machining precision and machine stability. Often resolved by proper levelling.
Physical harm to individuals or material harm to equipment/surroundings, often resulting from non-compliance with safety instructions or misuse.

relations.target: Tampering with Safety Devices is Forbidden
